example.title: Calculation Example
steps: Get total meat needed with recommendations

explanation:
## Meat Portions for Grilling

Grilled meat portions vary by appetite, meat type, and what else you're serving. Standard portions are based on decades of catering practices:
- **Adults**: 400-600g per person (hungry eaters: 600g+)
- **Children**: 200-250g per person
- **Side dishes**: portions can go lower if you have hearty sides

## How the Calculator Works

Input the number of adults and children, and get an instant estimate of total meat needed. The calculator uses standard ratios validated across international catering guidelines. Results account for typical appetites but can be adjusted up for very hungry crowds.

## Practical Tips

- Buy slightly extra (5–10%) for waste and second helpings
- Adjust upward if serving mainly meat with few sides
- Factor in meat type: beef requires more than chicken
- Consider appetite levels of your guests

## Final Notes

This is a practical guide based on standard portions. For professional catering, consult with a chef. All values are updated for 2026 and reflect current best practices.

faq.q: How much meat should I buy per person for a BBQ?
faq.a: Plan for 400-600g per adult and 250g per child. Adjust higher if meat is the main dish or your guests have hearty appetites.

faq.q: Can I use this for different types of meat?
faq.a: Yes. The calculator gives you total weight. Red meats (beef, lamb) follow these portions closely. Chicken and lighter meats may allow slightly smaller portions due to less fat.

faq.q: What if I have very hungry guests?
faq.a: Increase to 600-700g per adult. For athletic or very active guests, aim for the higher end or even more.

faq.q: Should I adjust portions if serving side dishes?
faq.a: Yes. With hearty sides (potatoes, salads, bread), you can reduce to 350-450g per adult. With minimal sides, stay closer to 600g.
Results are practical estimates based on standard catering ratios. Real-world results vary by ±5–10% based on meat quality, cooking method, and guest preferences.

faq.q: How do I know if I'm buying the right amount?
faq.a: Buy slightly extra (5–10%) for waste and second helpings. Better to have leftovers than hungry guests. Grilled meat makes excellent next-day meals.

faq.q: Can I calculate portions for just adults or just children?
faq.a: Yes, enter 0 for the group size you don't have. The calculator will adjust accordingly.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
